title: Usability Study for Flavor Encodings
consentText: **You are invited to participate in a research study on data visualization.**
The purpose of this study is to evaluate how different chart designs support interpretation of flavor-related data.
You will be asked to answer questions based on information shown in charts.
**The study will take approximately 8-12 minutes to complete.**
**We will record your responses and response times.**
**This study evaluates chart readability and task support, not personal ability.**
**Your participation is voluntary, and you may stop at any time.**
**All responses will be used for research purposes only and will remain anonymous.**
introText: In this study, you will view different chart types that represent flavor characteristics of foods.
Your task is to interpret the charts and answer questions about the information they display.
**The study is divided into two parts:**
**Part 1** focuses on understanding flavor summaries (mean values and variability).
**Part 2** focuses on comparing subgroup differences relative to a baseline.
Each part begins with a short tutorial, followed by practice trials and then main trials.
**Please answer based only on the information visible in the chart.**
**Work as accurately and quickly as possible.**

backgroundQuestions.label: How familiar are you with bar charts, violin plots, and radar charts?
How would you describe your data visualization experience?

blocks.title: Part 1: Flavor Summary
blocks.intro: This part evaluates how different chart types support understanding flavor summaries. You will identify mean values and variability across taste attributes.
blocks.taskInstruction: Select the best answer based only on the information visible in the chart.

onboarding.title: Grouped Bar Chart
callouts: Taller bars indicate larger mean ratings.

onboarding.title: Violin Plot
callouts: Wider regions indicate more ratings. Greater vertical spread indicates higher variability.

onboarding.title: Distribution-Aware Radial Profile
callouts: The outline shows the mean. The bands show how ratings are distributed.

practiceTrials.prompt: Which taste dimension has the highest mean rating for this food?
Which attribute shows the greatest variability in ratings?
Which taste dimension has the highest mean rating for this food?

realTrials.prompt: Which taste dimension has the highest mean rating for this food?
Which taste dimension has the highest mean rating for this food?
Which attribute shows the greatest variability in ratings?
Which attribute shows the greatest variability in ratings?
Which taste dimension has the highest mean rating for this food?
Which taste dimension has the highest mean rating for this food?
Which attribute shows the greatest variability in ratings?
Which attribute shows the greatest variability in ratings?

subjectiveSection.title: Chart Evaluation
subjectiveSection.instructions: Please rate each chart based on your experience in this part.

questions.label: This chart made it easy to identify the requested information.
I felt confident in my answers when using this chart.
This chart clearly showed differences across taste attributes.

blocks.title: Part 2: Subgroup Comparison
blocks.intro: This part evaluates how chart types support comparison between a subgroup and a baseline. You will identify differences across taste attributes.
blocks.taskInstruction: Select the best answer based only on the information visible in the chart.

onboarding.title: Overlaid Radar Chart
callouts: Compare the two profiles to identify differences across attributes.

onboarding.title: Z-Score Radar Chart
callouts: Direction shows above or below baseline. Larger displacement indicates larger differences.

practiceTrials.prompt: For this food, which attribute is rated higher by the subgroup than the baseline?
For this food, which attribute differs the most between the subgroup and the baseline?

realTrials.prompt: For this food, which attribute is rated higher by the subgroup than the baseline?
For this food, which attribute is rated higher by the subgroup than the baseline?
For this food, which attribute differs the most between the subgroup and the baseline?
For this food, which attribute differs the most between the subgroup and the baseline?
For this food, which attribute is rated higher by the subgroup than the baseline?
For this food, which attribute is rated higher by the subgroup than the baseline?
For this food, which attribute differs the most between the subgroup and the baseline?
For this food, which attribute differs the most between the subgroup and the baseline?

subjectiveSection.title: Chart Evaluation
subjectiveSection.instructions: Please rate each chart based on your experience in this part.

questions.label: This chart made it easy to identify differences between the subgroup and baseline.
I felt confident in my answers when using this chart.
This chart clearly showed how the subgroup differs from the baseline.

finalPreferenceQuestions.label: Which chart did you prefer for identifying highest mean values?
Which chart did you prefer for understanding variability?
Which chart did you prefer for comparing subgroup differences?
